metadata.description: Comprehensive electronics skill track from fundamentals through advanced topics, with prerequisite ordering

nodes.label: Voltage
nodes.description: Electric potential difference between two points. Measured in volts (V). Voltage sources, ground reference, and polarity conventions.

nodes.label: Current
nodes.description: Flow of electric charge through a conductor. Measured in amperes (A). Conventional vs electron flow, DC vs AC current.

nodes.label: Resistance
nodes.description: Opposition to current flow. Measured in ohms. Material resistivity, conductors vs insulators, temperature effects on resistance.

nodes.label: Ohm's Law
nodes.description: V = IR relationship between voltage, current, and resistance. Applying Ohm's law to calculate unknown quantities in simple circuits.

nodes.label: Electrical Power
nodes.description: Power dissipation in circuits: P = VI = I²R = V²/R. Watts, energy consumption, and thermal considerations.

nodes.label: Series & Parallel Circuits
nodes.description: Series circuits share current; parallel circuits share voltage. Calculating equivalent resistance, voltage dividers, and current dividers.

nodes.label: Kirchhoff's Laws
nodes.description: KVL: sum of voltages around a loop equals zero. KCL: sum of currents at a node equals zero. Systematic circuit analysis.

nodes.label: DC vs AC
nodes.description: Direct current vs alternating current. Frequency, amplitude, phase, RMS values, and peak-to-peak measurements.

nodes.label: Using a Multimeter
nodes.description: Measuring voltage, current, and resistance with a digital multimeter. Continuity testing, proper probe placement, and safety.

nodes.label: Breadboard Prototyping
nodes.description: Solderless breadboard layout and connections. Building basic circuits, power rails, and common prototyping practices.

nodes.label: Resistors
nodes.description: Resistor types (carbon, metal film, wirewound), color code reading, power ratings, tolerance, and applications in circuits.

nodes.label: Potentiometers & Variable Resistors
nodes.description: Variable resistance devices. Linear vs logarithmic taper, trimpots, rheostats, and voltage divider applications.

nodes.label: Capacitors
nodes.description: Energy storage in electric fields. Capacitance (farads), types (ceramic, electrolytic, film), charging/discharging behavior, and RC time constants.

nodes.label: Inductors
nodes.description: Energy storage in magnetic fields. Inductance (henrys), coil construction, RL time constants, and back-EMF.

nodes.label: Transformers
nodes.description: Electromagnetic induction between coils. Turns ratio, step-up/step-down, isolation transformers, and power transfer efficiency.

nodes.label: Impedance & Reactance
nodes.description: AC resistance of capacitors and inductors. Capacitive and inductive reactance, impedance as complex quantity, phasor diagrams.

nodes.label: Resonance
nodes.description: LC resonant circuits. Natural frequency, series vs parallel resonance, quality factor (Q), and bandwidth.

nodes.label: Diodes
nodes.description: PN junction operation. Forward/reverse bias, forward voltage drop, rectification, signal diodes vs power diodes.

nodes.label: Special Diodes
nodes.description: Zener diodes (voltage regulation), Schottky diodes (fast switching), LEDs (light emission), photodiodes (light detection), and varactor diodes.

nodes.label: Bipolar Junction Transistors (BJT)
nodes.description: NPN and PNP transistors. Current gain (hFE/beta), saturation and cutoff regions, transistor as switch, and biasing techniques.

nodes.label: MOSFETs
nodes.description: Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor FETs. N-channel and P-channel, enhancement vs depletion mode, gate threshold voltage, high-speed switching.

nodes.label: Op-Amp Basics
nodes.description: Operational amplifier fundamentals. Ideal op-amp model, open-loop gain, input impedance, virtual short concept, and common IC packages (LM741, LM358).

nodes.label: Op-Amp Configurations
nodes.description: Inverting, non-inverting, voltage follower, summing, and difference amplifier configurations. Gain calculations and feedback networks.

nodes.label: Thyristors & Triacs
nodes.description: SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) operation and triggering. Triacs for AC switching, gate control, and phase angle control.

nodes.label: Voltage Regulators
nodes.description: Linear regulators (78xx, LDO), dropout voltage, thermal management, and basic regulation concepts.

nodes.label: Transistor Amplifiers
nodes.description: Common emitter, common collector (emitter follower), and common base amplifier topologies. Voltage gain, input/output impedance, and coupling capacitors.

nodes.label: Differential Amplifiers
nodes.description: Differential pair topology, common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR), instrumentation amplifiers, and balanced signal processing.

nodes.label: Passive Filters
nodes.description: RC, RL, and RLC low-pass, high-pass, band-pass, and band-stop filters. Cutoff frequency, roll-off, and Bode plots.

nodes.label: Active Filters
nodes.description: Op-amp based Sallen-Key, Butterworth, and Chebyshev filter designs. Higher-order filters, filter design tools, and frequency response tuning.

nodes.label: Oscillators
nodes.description: RC oscillators (Wien bridge, phase shift), LC oscillators (Colpitts, Hartley), crystal oscillators, and Barkhausen stability criterion.

nodes.label: 555 Timer Circuits
nodes.description: 555 timer IC in astable, monostable, and bistable modes. Duty cycle control, frequency calculation, and practical applications.

nodes.label: Linear Power Supplies
nodes.description: Transformer, rectifier (half/full bridge), filter capacitor, and regulator stages. Ripple calculation and load regulation.

nodes.label: Audio Amplifier Circuits
nodes.description: Class A, B, AB, and D audio amplifiers. Pre-amplifier stages, power output stages, speaker matching, and distortion minimization.

nodes.label: Comparators & Schmitt Triggers
nodes.description: Voltage comparator circuits, hysteresis with Schmitt triggers, window comparators, and level detection applications.

nodes.label: Number Systems
nodes.description: Binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal number systems. Conversions between bases, binary arithmetic, and two's complement representation.

nodes.label: Logic Gates
nodes.description: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, and XNOR gates. Truth tables, gate symbols, and TTL/CMOS IC implementations (74xx series).

nodes.label: Boolean Algebra
nodes.description: Boolean laws and theorems (De Morgan's, absorption, distributive). Simplification of logic expressions and canonical forms (SOP, POS).

nodes.label: Karnaugh Maps
nodes.description: K-map method for simplifying Boolean expressions. 2, 3, and 4-variable maps, don't-care conditions, and minimal expressions.

nodes.label: Combinational Logic Circuits
nodes.description: Adders (half, full, ripple carry), subtractors, encoders, decoders, and parity generators. Multi-level gate networks.

nodes.label: Multiplexers & Demultiplexers
nodes.description: MUX and DEMUX circuits. Data selection, address lines, cascading multiplexers, and implementing Boolean functions with MUX.

nodes.label: Flip-Flops & Latches
nodes.description: SR, D, JK, and T flip-flops. Latch vs flip-flop, edge triggering, setup/hold times, and clock signals.

nodes.label: Counters
nodes.description: Asynchronous (ripple) and synchronous counters. Up/down counters, modulo-N counters, ring counters, and Johnson counters.

nodes.label: Shift Registers
nodes.description: SISO, SIPO, PISO, and PIPO shift registers. Serial-to-parallel conversion, data buffering, and shift register applications.

nodes.label: Memory (RAM, ROM, Flash)
nodes.description: Memory types: SRAM, DRAM, ROM, EEPROM, and Flash. Memory addressing, read/write cycles, and memory hierarchy basics.

nodes.label: Finite State Machines
nodes.description: Mealy and Moore state machines. State diagrams, state tables, state assignment, and sequential circuit design methodology.

nodes.label: Arduino Introduction
nodes.description: Arduino platform overview. Board variants (Uno, Nano, Mega), IDE setup, sketch structure (setup/loop), and uploading code.

nodes.label: GPIO (Digital I/O)
nodes.description: General purpose input/output pins. Digital read/write, pull-up/pull-down resistors, driving LEDs, reading buttons and switches.

nodes.label: ADC (Analog to Digital)
nodes.description: Analog-to-digital conversion. Resolution (10-bit, 12-bit), reference voltage, sampling rate, reading analog sensors.

nodes.label: DAC (Digital to Analog)
nodes.description: Digital-to-analog conversion. R-2R ladder, external DAC modules, generating analog waveforms from digital signals.

nodes.label: PWM (Pulse Width Modulation)
nodes.description: PWM signal generation. Duty cycle, frequency control, LED dimming, motor speed control, and servo positioning.

nodes.label: Interrupts
nodes.description: Hardware and software interrupts. ISR (Interrupt Service Routine), external interrupts, timer interrupts, priority, and debouncing.

nodes.label: Timers & Counters
nodes.description: Hardware timer/counter peripherals. Prescalers, timer modes (CTC, fast PWM), watchdog timer, and timing-critical applications.

nodes.label: Serial Communication Basics
nodes.description: Serial vs parallel communication. Baud rate, data framing, serial monitor debugging, and basic UART communication.

nodes.label: LCD & OLED Displays
nodes.description: Character LCD (HD44780), graphic OLED (SSD1306), display libraries, custom characters, and I2C display interfaces.

nodes.label: Motor Control with MCU
nodes.description: Driving DC motors, stepper motors, and servos from microcontrollers. H-bridge drivers (L298N, L293D), motor shields, and PID speed control.

nodes.label: UART Protocol
nodes.description: Universal Asynchronous Receiver-Transmitter. Frame format, baud rate, start/stop bits, parity, RS-232 and TTL levels.

nodes.label: SPI Protocol
nodes.description: Serial Peripheral Interface. Master/slave, MOSI/MISO/SCK/CS lines, clock polarity/phase modes, and daisy chaining.

nodes.label: I2C Protocol
nodes.description: Inter-Integrated Circuit bus. SDA/SCL lines, addressing, start/stop conditions, clock stretching, and multi-device buses.

nodes.label: WiFi (ESP8266/ESP32)
nodes.description: WiFi connectivity with ESP modules. Station and AP modes, HTTP requests, MQTT protocol, and IoT cloud integration.

nodes.label: Bluetooth & BLE
nodes.description: Bluetooth Classic and BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy). HC-05/HC-06 modules, BLE services/characteristics, and mobile app communication.

nodes.label: RF Basics
nodes.description: Radio frequency fundamentals. Frequency bands, modulation (AM, FM, digital), antennas, nRF24L01 modules, and LoRa for long range.

nodes.label: CAN Bus
nodes.description: Controller Area Network protocol. Differential signaling, message IDs, arbitration, automotive and industrial applications.

nodes.label: USB Fundamentals
nodes.description: USB protocol overview. USB types (2.0, 3.0, Type-C), device classes, enumeration, HID devices, and CDC serial emulation.

nodes.label: Schematic Capture
nodes.description: Drawing circuit schematics in EDA tools (KiCad, Eagle). Component symbols, net labels, hierarchical sheets, and design rule checks.

nodes.label: Component Footprints
nodes.description: PCB footprint libraries. Through-hole vs SMD packages, pad dimensions, courtyard areas, and creating custom footprints.

nodes.label: PCB Layout
nodes.description: Component placement on PCB. Board outline, mounting holes, layer stackup, ground planes, and thermal relief pads.

nodes.label: Trace Routing
nodes.description: Copper trace routing. Track width calculations, via types, differential pairs, impedance control, and auto-router vs manual routing.

nodes.label: Design Rules & DRC
nodes.description: Design Rule Check configuration. Clearances, minimum trace widths, via constraints, manufacturer specifications, and Gerber output.

nodes.label: Soldering Techniques
nodes.description: Through-hole and basic SMD soldering. Iron temperature, flux, tinning, desoldering (solder wick, pump), and joint inspection.

nodes.label: SMD Assembly
nodes.description: Surface Mount Device assembly. Solder paste stenciling, pick-and-place basics, reflow soldering, hot air rework, and component sizes (0402, 0603, 0805).

nodes.label: Testing & Debugging PCBs
nodes.description: Board bring-up process. Visual inspection, continuity testing, power rail verification, oscilloscope probing, and logic analyzers.

nodes.label: PCB Fabrication & Ordering
nodes.description: Generating Gerber files, BOM, and pick-and-place files. Ordering from manufacturers (JLCPCB, PCBWay), panelization, and assembly services.

nodes.label: Digital Signal Processing Basics
nodes.description: Sampling theorem, Nyquist frequency, aliasing, quantization. FIR and IIR digital filters, FFT, and DSP on microcontrollers.

nodes.label: EMC / EMI
nodes.description: Electromagnetic compatibility and interference. Shielding, grounding techniques, decoupling capacitors, PCB layout for EMC, and regulatory standards.

nodes.label: Switching Power Supplies (SMPS)
nodes.description: Buck, boost, and buck-boost converter topologies. PWM control, inductor selection, output filtering, efficiency, and IC-based designs.

nodes.label: Motor Drives & Power Electronics
nodes.description: BLDC motor control, VFDs (variable frequency drives), IGBT gate drivers, regenerative braking, and high-power switching.

nodes.label: RTOS & Embedded OS
nodes.description: Real-Time Operating Systems (FreeRTOS). Tasks, scheduling, semaphores, mutexes, queues, and deterministic timing for embedded applications.

nodes.label: ARM Cortex Processors
nodes.description: ARM Cortex-M architecture. STM32 family, register-level programming, HAL libraries, debugging with SWD/JTAG, and bare-metal development.

nodes.label: FPGA Introduction
nodes.description: Field-Programmable Gate Arrays. HDL (Verilog/VHDL) basics, synthesis, look-up tables, and FPGA development boards.

nodes.label: Battery Management Systems
nodes.description: Battery chemistries (Li-ion, LiPo, NiMH), charging circuits (CC-CV), cell balancing, protection circuits, and fuel gauging.

nodes.label: IoT System Design
nodes.description: End-to-end IoT architecture. Sensor nodes, edge computing, cloud platforms (AWS IoT, ThingSpeak), OTA updates, and low-power design.

nodes.label: Mixed-Signal Design
nodes.description: Combining analog and digital on one PCB. Grounding strategies, signal integrity, guard traces, analog/digital partitioning, and noise reduction.
